mnemonics: Finally, I always run! (Like a loyal friend who always shows up, the finally block always executes regardless of what happens in the try or catch blocks.)
keyPoints: A finally block always executes when the try block exits, ensuring necessary cleanup code is run.
It executes even if an exception is thrown or a return statement is encountered within the try or catch blocks.
It is typically used to close resources like files, network connections, or database connections to prevent leaks.
commonMistakes: Placing a return statement inside the finally block, which can suppress exceptions thrown in the try or catch blocks.
Assuming the finally block won't execute if System.exit() is called (it won't, as the program terminates immediately).
